title: Eligibility, Entitlement & Employment Handicap Builder
desc: Build your strategy and evidence portfolio to prove basic eligibility, vocational impairment, and Serious Employment Handicap needs.

ehIndicators.label: Currently unemployed or unable to secure stable work
Underemployed (working in a job below skill level or potential)
Current job aggravates service-connected medical conditions
Frequent job changes or recent job losses due to limitations
Unable to perform prior primary civilian or military occupation
Requires significant physical or cognitive workplace accommodations
Lacks the training, credentials, or licenses for a suitable career
Active medical restrictions on lifting, standing, sitting, or cognitive workload
Failed attempts at prior training programs or university courses due to symptoms

sehIndicators.label: Multiple severe rating decisions (e.g. combined orthopedic, mental, or neurological)
Severe functional restrictions on major daily activities
Repeated failed attempts to sustain suitable employment
Requires extensive or specialized training (e.g. grad school, specialized equipment)
Needs Independent Living services to perform basic family/community tasks
Prior Chapter 31 case was interrupted or discontinued without successful placement
Requires more than 48 months of training to achieve vocational recovery
Medical instability causing frequent absences or disruptions in training/work

feasibilityFactors.label: My medical conditions are stable enough to allow active participation in a training program
I have identified the specific accommodations needed to handle training and coursework
A treating physician has confirmed I can safely train for this specific vocational goal
The VRC has expressed doubt about my feasibility, and I want an Extended Evaluation

vaErrors.title: Telling 10% rated Veterans they are ineligible for Chapter 31 services
vaErrors.explanation: A 10% rating is legally eligible to apply. Although it requires a finding of a Serious Employment Handicap (SEH) under 38 C.F.R. § 21.52 to establish entitlement, they cannot be summarily denied applications without that clinical evaluation.

vaErrors.title: Treating a 20%+ rating as automatic entitlement without vocational handicap evaluation
vaErrors.explanation: A 20% rating establishes basic eligibility, but entitlement is not automatic. The VRC must evaluate whether the service-connected disability contributes to an employment handicap (vocational impairment not yet overcome).

vaErrors.title: Asserting that current employment automatically defeats an Employment Handicap
vaErrors.explanation: Being employed does not bar entitlement. If the current job is unsuitable (e.g., underemployed, aggravates disabilities, or lacks growth potential), the veteran retains entitlement under § 21.51.

vaErrors.title: Denying EH or SEH without conducting a separate, detailed clinical analysis
vaErrors.explanation: VRCs cannot issue standard denials without evaluating the veteran's vocational impairment and resolving doubts in favor of the veteran under 38 C.F.R. § 21.57.
